political.s: <cite index="1-2,1-3">Wells Fargo contributed $4.7M and spent $4.55M on lobbying in 2024</cite>, reflecting bipartisan engagement with both Democratic and Republican recipients. <cite index="7-1,7-2">The bank has tripled political donations to state groups over the past decade, contributing $704.3K to state-focused 527 organizations in the 2024 cycle</cite>. <cite index="4-8,4-9">After pledging to consider voting records on the Electoral College, Wells Fargo PAC later donated to Republican objectors despite this commitment</cite>.

charity.s: <cite index="11-2">Wells Fargo is donating roughly $420 million through its Open for Business Fund to help small businesses</cite>. <cite index="11-16,11-17">The Wells Fargo Foundation is committed to helping communities thrive by supporting financially resilient families, vibrant small businesses, and affordable housing, with grantmaking focused on financial opportunity, housing access and affordability, and small business growth</cite>. The company supports employee volunteerism through multiple programs.
charity.amt: ~$420M+ (Open for Business Fund) plus foundation grants
charity.focus: Financial opportunity, housing affordability, small business growth

environment.s: <cite index="21-6,21-7">Wells Fargo has a $500 billion sustainable finance goal by 2030 and has deployed $264 billion, over halfway to its goal, with 15% Community Finance and 23% Environmental Finance</cite>. However, <cite index="28-1,28-14,28-15">the bank previously pledged to achieve net zero greenhouse gas emissions in its financing activities by 2050 but has now scrapped its goal to deliver net zero financed emissions by 2050 and discontinued its sector-specific 2030 interim targets</cite>. <cite index="22-5">Wells Fargo's InfluenceMap relationship score of 43 out of 100 and overall grade of D+ reflect negative trade association engagement</cite>.

labor.s: <cite index="31-1,31-8,31-9,31-10">Over the past year, Wells Fargo employees at several offices filed unfair labor practice charges with the NLRB; in May 2023, the company settled a case involving a manager threatening pro-union workers at its Salt Lake City call center, and the NLRB investigated three additional ULP cases at its Beaverton, Oregon call center</cite>. <cite index="36-2,36-3">OSHA found Wells Fargo violated Sarbanes-Oxley whistleblower protections by improperly terminating a senior manager and ordered the company to pay the employee more than $22 million</cite>. <cite index="37-2">Wells Fargo was charged with secretly recording and transcribing union contract negotiations, violating the National Labor Relations Act</cite>.

dei.s: <cite index="41-3,41-4">In late May 2025, Wells Fargo's DEI landing page and related content disappeared from its website, with the company previously displaying annual DEI reports, racial equity assessments, and pay equity analysis</cite>. <cite index="45-8">The change followed a February 2025 policy shift when Wells Fargo announced it would no longer require hiring managers to consider a diverse group of candidates for senior-level positions</cite>. <cite index="45-1,45-2,45-3">Despite public changes, Wells Fargo has maintained internal inclusion programs, with Ebony Thomas continuing as head of DEI and employee resource groups remaining active</cite>.

privacy.s: <cite index="59-2,59-3">Wells Fargo does not sell personal data but may share personal data for purposes of cross-context behavioral advertising</cite>. <cite index="52-1,52-2,52-3">California consumers have rights to delete personal data, correct inaccurate information, and opt out of sharing for behavioral advertising purposes</cite>. <cite index="51-13,51-14">Wells Fargo and its service providers worldwide may access customer information, with protective measures maintained as described in privacy policies and notices</cite>.

execPay.s: <cite index="63-1">CEO Charles Scharf's 2024 pay ratio was $378 to $1, with median employee compensation at $80,296</cite>. <cite index="64-4,64-5">For 2025, Wells Fargo approved $40 million in compensation for Scharf, representing an increase of more than 28% compared with his $31.2 million total in 2024</cite>. <cite index="70-1,70-3">The $40 million 2025 package represents the highest level for a Wells Fargo top leader in decades, consisting of $2.5 million base salary and $37.5 million in bonus</cite>.

fec.committeeName: WELLS FARGO AND COMPANY EMPLOYEE PAC (AKA WELLS FARGO EMPLOYEE PAC)

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
